87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: For such the requital is the curse of God and the angels and of men.

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: The recompense of such is that on them is the curse of Allah and of the angels and of men combined.

87.author: Arberry
87.text: Those -- their recompense is that there shall rest on them the curse of God and of the angels and of men, altogether,

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: These! their meed is that on them shall be the curse of Allah and of angles and of mankind, all;

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: They are those whose recompense is that on them (rests) the Curse of Allah, of the angels, and of all mankind.

87.author: Itani
87.text: Those—their penalty is that upon them falls the curse of God, and of the angels, and of all mankind.

87.author: Maududi
87.text: The recom- pense for their wrong-doing is that the curse of Allah and of the angels and of all men shall upon them.

87.author: Mubarakpuri
87.text: They are those whose recompense is that on them (rests) the curse of Allah, of the angels and of all mankind.

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: As for such, their guerdon is that on them rests the curse of Allah and of angels and of men combined.

87.author: Qarai
87.text: Their requital is that there shall be upon them the curse of Allah, the angels, and all mankind.

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: Those, their recompense shall be the curse of Allah, the angels, and all the people

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: Those - their recompense will be that upon them is the curse of Allah and the angels and the people, all together,

87.author: Sarwar
87.text: What they will gain will be the condemnation of God, the angels, and all people,

87.author: Shakir
87.text: (As for) these, their reward is that upon them is the curse of Allah and the angels and of men, all together.

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: such people will be rewarded with rejection by God, by the angels, by all mankind.

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: Of such the reward is that on them (rests) the curse of Allah, of His angels, and of all mankind;-

87.author: Transliteration
87.text: Olaika jazaohum anna AAalayhim laAAnata Allahi waalmalaikati waalnnasi ajmaAAeena

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: They shall live under it, and none of their agony decrease nor be respite for them.

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: Remaining in it forever; their punishment will not be lightened, nor will they get any respite.

88.author: Arberry
88.text: therein dwelling forever; the chastisement shall not be lightened for them; no respite shall be given them.

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: Abiders therein, their torment shall not be lightened nor shall they be respited.

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: They will abide therein (Hell). Neither will their torment be lightened, nor will it be delayed or postponed (for a while).

88.author: Itani
88.text: Remaining in it eternally, without their punishment being eased from them, and without being reprieved.

88.author: Maududi
88.text: Thus shall they abide. Neither shall their chastisement be granted nor shall they be granted any respite.

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: They will abide therein. Neither will their torment be lightened nor will it be delayed or postponed.

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: They will abide therein. Their doom will not be lightened, neither will they be reprieved;

88.author: Qarai
88.text: They will remain in it [forever], and their punishment will not be lightened, nor will they be granted any respite,

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: there they shall live for ever. Their punishment shall not be lightened, nor shall they be given respite.

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: Abiding eternally therein. The punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be reprieved.

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: with which they will live forever. Their torment will not be relieved and no attention will be paid to them.

88.author: Shakir
88.text: Abiding in it; their chastisement shall not be lightened nor shall they be respited.

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: In this state they shall abide forever; their punishment shall not be lightened nor shall they be granted respite.

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: In that will they dwell; nor will their penalty be lightened, nor respite be (their lot);-

88.author: Transliteration
88.text: Khalideena feeha la yukhaffafu AAanhumu alAAathabu wala hum yuntharoona

89.author: Ahmed Ali
89.text: But those who repent and reform, God is surely forgiving and merciful.

89.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
89.text: Except those who repented thereafter and reformed themselves; then indeed Allah is Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.

89.author: Arberry
89.text: But those who repent thereafter, and make amends -- God is All-forgiving, All-compassionate.

89.author: Daryabadi
89.text: Save such shall repent thereafter and amend; verily Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

89.author: Hilali & Khan
89.text: Except for those who repent after that and do righteous deeds. Verily, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

89.author: Itani
89.text: Except those who repent afterwards, and reform; for God is Forgiving and Merciful.

89.author: Maududi
89.text: But those who repent and mend their ways shall be excepted for indeed Allah is Forgiving, All-Compassionate.

89.author: Mubarakpuri
89.text: Except for those who repent after that and do righteous deeds. Verily, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

89.author: Pickthall
89.text: Save those who afterward repent and do right. Lo! Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

89.author: Qarai
89.text: except such as repent after that and make amends, for Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

89.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
89.text: Except those who afterwards repent and mend their ways, Allah is Forgiving and the Most Merciful.

89.author: Saheeh International
89.text: Except for those who repent after that and correct themselves. For indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.

89.author: Sarwar
89.text: However, to those who repent afterwards and reform themselves, God is All-forgiving and All-merciful.

89.author: Shakir
89.text: Except those who repent after that and amend, then surely Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

89.author: Wahiduddin Khan
89.text: Except for those who afterwards repent and reform. God is forgiving and merciful;

89.author: Yusuf Ali
89.text: Except for those that repent (Even) after that, and make amends; for verily Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

89.author: Transliteration
89.text: Illa allatheena taboo min baAAdi thalika waaslahoo fainna Allaha ghafoorun raheemun

90.author: Ahmed Ali
90.text: Those who deny, having once come to faith, and persist in denial, will not have their repentance accepted, for they have gone astray.

90.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
90.text: Indeed those who disbelieve after having accepted faith, and advance further in their disbelief – their repentance will never be accepted; and it is they who are the astray.

90.author: Arberry
90.text: Surely those who disbelieve after they have believed and then increase in unbelief -- their repentance shall not be accepted; those are the ones who stray.

90.author: Daryabadi
90.text: Verily those who disbelieve after they have believed and thereafter wax in infidelity, their- repentance shall by no means be accepted. These! they are the straying ones.

90.author: Hilali & Khan
90.text: Verily, those who disbelieved after their Belief and then went on increasing in their disbelief (i.e. disbelief in the Quran and in Prophet Muhammad SAW) - never will their repentance be accepted [because they repent only by their tongues and not from their hearts]. And they are those who are astray.

90.author: Itani
90.text: As for those who disbelieve after having believed, then plunge deeper into disbelief, their repentance will not be accepted; these are the lost.

90.author: Maududi
90.text: Those who relieved and have har-dened in their disbelief after once believing, their (pretence to) repentance shall not be accepted. Indeed such men have altogether strayed.

90.author: Mubarakpuri
90.text: Verily, those who disbelieved after their belief and then went on increasing in their disbelief never will their repentance be accepted. And they are those who are astray.

90.author: Pickthall
90.text: Lo! those who disbelieve after their (profession of) belief, and afterward grow violent in disbelief: their repentance will not be accepted. And such are those who are astray.

90.author: Qarai
90.text: Indeed those who turn faithless after their faith, and then advance in faithlessness, their repentance will never be accepted, and it is they who are the astray.

90.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
90.text: Indeed those who disbelieve after they have believed and increase in disbelief, their repentance shall not be accepted. These are those who are astray.

90.author: Saheeh International
90.text: Indeed, those who reject the message after their belief and then increase in disbelief - never will their [claimed] repentance be accepted, and they are the ones astray.

90.author: Sarwar
90.text: Those who disbelieve, after having been believers and increase their disbelief, are lost and their repentance will not be accepted.

90.author: Shakir
90.text: Surely, those who disbelieve after their believing, then increase in unbelief, their repentance shall not be accepted, and these are they that go astray.

90.author: Wahiduddin Khan
90.text: but as for those who are bent on denying the truth after accepting the true faith and grow in their refusal to acknowledge the truth, their repentance will not be accepted. They are the ones who have gone far astray.

90.author: Yusuf Ali
90.text: But those who reject Faith after they accepted it, and then go on adding to their defiance of Faith,- never will their repentance be accepted; for they are those who have (of set purpose) gone astray.

90.author: Transliteration
90.text: Inna allatheena kafaroo baAAda eemanihim thumma izdadoo kufran lan tuqbala tawbatuhum waolaika humu alddalloona

91.author: Ahmed Ali
91.text: From those who deny and die disbelieving will never be accepted an earthful of gold if proferred by them as ransom. For them is grievous punishment, and none will help them.

91.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
91.text: Those who disbelieved and died as disbelievers – an earth full of gold will never be accepted from any one of them, even if he offers it, for his freedom; for them is a painful punishment and they do not have any aides.

91.author: Arberry
91.text: Surely those who disbelieve, and die disbelieving, there shall not be accepted from any one of them the whole earth full of gold, if he would ransom himself thereby; for them awaits a painful chastisement, and they shall have no helpers.

91.author: Daryabadi
91.text: Verily those who disbelieve and die while they are infidels, not an earthful of gold shall be accepted from any such, were he to offer it as a ransom. These! theirs shall be a torment afflictive, nor shall they have any helpers.

91.author: Hilali & Khan
91.text: Verily, those who disbelieved, and died while they were disbelievers, the (whole) earth full of gold will not be accepted from anyone of them even if they offered it as a ransom. For them is a painful torment and they will have no helpers.

91.author: Itani
91.text: As for those who disbelieve and die disbelievers, even the earth full of gold would not be accepted from any of them, were he to offer it for ransom. These will have a painful torment, and will have no saviors.

91.author: Maududi
91.text: Truly those who disbelieved and died as unbelievers, not even an earth full of gold will be accepted from them as ransom. For such people there is painful chastisement; and none shall come to their help.

91.author: Mubarakpuri
91.text: Verily, those who disbelieved, and died while they were disbelievers, the (whole) earth full of gold will not be accepted from anyone of them if they offered it as a ransom. For them is a painful torment and they will have no helpers.

91.author: Pickthall
91.text: Lo! those who disbelieve, and die in disbelief, the (whole) earth full of gold would not be accepted from such an one if it were offered as a ransom (for his soul). Theirs will be a painful doom and they will have no helpers.

91.author: Qarai
91.text: Indeed those who turn faithless and die while they are faithless, a world of gold will not be accepted from any of them should he offer it for ransom. For such there will be a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers.

91.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
91.text: As for those who disbelieve and die unbelievers, there shall be not accepted from anyone of them, the whole earth full of gold, if he would ransom himself thereby. Those, for them there is a painful punishment, and none shall help them.

91.author: Saheeh International
91.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers - never would the [whole] capacity of the earth in gold be accepted from one of them if he would [seek to] ransom himself with it. For those there will be a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers.

91.author: Sarwar
91.text: From those who have rejected the truth and died in disbelief, no ransom will be accepted even though they may pay a whole earth of gold. They will suffer a painful torment and no one will help them.

91.author: Shakir
91.text: Surely, those who disbelieve and die while they are unbelievers, the earth full of gold shall not be accepted from one of them, though he should offer to ransom himself with it, these it is who shall have a painful chastisement, and they shall have no helpers.

91.author: Wahiduddin Khan
91.text: Those who reject faith and die in the state of rejection will not be saved, even if they offer as ransom enough gold to fill the entire earth. Painful punishment is in store for them and they will have no supporters.

91.author: Yusuf Ali
91.text: As to those who reject Faith, and die rejecting,- never would be accepted from any such as much gold as the earth contains, though they should offer it for ransom. For such is (in store) a penalty grievous, and they will find no helpers.

91.author: Transliteration
91.text: Inna allatheena kafaroo wamatoo wahum kuffarun falan yuqbala min ahadihim milo alardi thahaban walawi iftada bihi olaika lahum AAathabun aleemun wama lahum min nasireena

92.author: Ahmed Ali
92.text: You will never come to piety unless you spend of things you love; and whatever you spend is known to God.

92.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
92.text: You can never attain virtue until you spend in Allah's cause the things you love; and Allah is Aware of whatever you spend.

92.author: Arberry
92.text: You will not attain piety until you expend of what you love; and whatever thing you expend, God knows of it.

92.author: Daryabadi
92.text: O Ye shall not attain unto virtue until ye expend of that which ye love; and whatsoever ye expend, verily Allah Is thereof Knower.

92.author: Hilali & Khan
92.text: By no means shall you attain Al-Birr (piety, righteousness, etc., it means here Allah's Reward, i.e. Paradise), unless you spend (in Allah's Cause) of that which you love; and whatever of good you spend, Allah knows it well.

92.author: Itani
92.text: You will not attain virtuous conduct until you give of what you cherish. Whatever you give away, God is aware of it.

92.author: Maududi
92.text: You shall not attain righteousness until you spend out of what you love (in the way of Allah). Allah knows whatever you spend.

92.author: Mubarakpuri
92.text: By no means shall you attain Al-Birr, unless you spend of that which you love; and whatever of good you spend, Allah knows it well.

92.author: Pickthall
92.text: Ye will not attain unto piety until ye spend of that which ye love. And whatsoever ye spend, Allah is Aware thereof.

92.author: Qarai
92.text: You will never attain piety until you spend out of what you hold dear, and whatever you may spend of anything, Allah indeed knows it.

92.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
92.text: You shall not attain righteousness until you spend of what you love. Whatever you spend is known to Allah.

92.author: Saheeh International
92.text: Never will you attain the good [reward] until you spend [in the way of Allah] from that which you love. And whatever you spend - indeed, Allah is Knowing of it.

92.author: Sarwar
92.text: You can never have extended virtue and righteousness unless you spend part of what you dearly love for the cause of God. God knows very well whatever you spend for His cause.

92.author: Shakir
92.text: By no means shall you attain to righteousness until you spend (benevolently) out of what you love; and whatever thing you spend, Allah surely knows it.

92.author: Wahiduddin Khan
92.text: Never will you attain to righteousness unless you spend for the cause of God out of what you cherish; and whatever you spend is known to God.

92.author: Yusuf Ali
92.text: By no means shall ye attain righteousness unless ye give (freely) of that which ye love; and whatever ye give, of a truth Allah knoweth it well.

92.author: Transliteration
92.text: Lan tanaloo albirra hatta tunfiqoo mimma tuhibboona wama tunfiqoo min shayin fainna Allaha bihi AAaleemun

93.author: Ahmed Ali
93.text: To the children of Israel was lawful all food except what Israel forbade himself before the Torah was revealed. Say: "Bring the Torah and recite it, if what you say is true."

93.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
93.text: All food was lawful for the Descendants of Israel (Jacob), except what Israel forbade for himself, before the Taurat (Torah) was sent down; say, “Bring the Taurat and read it, if you are truthful.”

93.author: Arberry
93.text: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel save what Israel forbade for himself before the Torah was sent down. Say: 'Bring you the Torah now, and recite it, if you are truthful.'

93.author: Daryabadi
93.text: Every foods was allowable unto the Children of Isra'il, save that which Isra'il had forbidden unto himself, ere the Taurat was revealed. Say thou: bring ye then the Taurat and recite it, if ye say sooth.

93.author: Hilali & Khan
93.text: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel, except what Israel made unlawful for himself before the Taurat (Torah) was revealed. Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Bring here the Taurat (Torah) and recite it, if you are truthful."

93.author: Itani
93.text: All food was permissible to the Children of Israel, except what Israel forbade for itself before the Torah was revealed. Say, “Bring the Torah, and read it, if you are truthful.”

93.author: Maududi
93.text: All food (that is lawful in the Law revealed to Muhammad) was lawful to the Children of Israel, except what Israel made unlawful to themselves before the revelation of the Torah. Tell them: 'Bring the Torah and recite any passage of it if you are truthful.'

93.author: Mubarakpuri
93.text: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel, except what Isra'il made unlawful for himself before the Tawrah was revealed. Say: "Bring here the Tawrah and recite it, if you are truthful. "

93.author: Pickthall
93.text: All food was lawful unto the Children of Israel, save that which Israel forbade himself, (in days) before the Torah was revealed. Say: Produce the Torah and read it (unto us) if ye are truthful.

93.author: Qarai
93.text: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel had forbidden himself before the Torah was sent down. Say, ‘Bring the Torah, and read it, if you are truthful.’

93.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
93.text: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel forbade himself before the Torah had been sent down. Say: 'Bring the Torah and recite it, if you are truthful'

93.author: Saheeh International
93.text: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel had made unlawful to himself before the Torah was revealed. Say, [O Muhammad], "So bring the Torah and recite it, if you should be truthful."

93.author: Sarwar
93.text: All food was lawful for the children of Israel except for what Israel had deemed unlawful for himself before the Torah was revealed. (Muhammad), ask them to bring the Torah and read it, if they are true in their claim (that all food was not unlawful for the children of Israel).

93.author: Shakir
93.text: All food was lawful to the children of Israel except that which Israel had forbidden to himself, before the Taurat was revealed. Say: Bring then the Taurat and read it, if you are truthful.

93.author: Wahiduddin Khan
93.text: All food was lawful for the Children of Israel, except whatever Israel had made unlawful for himself before the Torah was sent down. Say to them, "Bring the Torah then and read it, if you are truthful.

93.author: Yusuf Ali
93.text: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel, except what Israel Made unlawful for itself, before the Law (of Moses) was revealed. Say: "Bring ye the Law and study it, if ye be men of truth."

93.author: Transliteration
93.text: Kullu alttaAAami kana hillan libanee israeela illa ma harrama israeelu AAala nafsihi min qabli an tunazzala alttawratu qul fatoo bialttawrati faotlooha in kuntum sadiqeena

94.author: Ahmed Ali
94.text: And anyone who fabricates lies about God even after this, is wicked indeed.

94.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
94.text: So henceforth whoever fabricates lies against Allah – it is they who are the unjust.

94.author: Arberry
94.text: Whoso forges falsehood against God after that, those are the evildoers.

94.author: Daryabadi
94.text: Then whosoever fabricateth after this, lie against Allah - these! they are the wrong-doers.

94.author: Hilali & Khan
94.text: Then after that, whosoever shall invent a lie against Allah,   such shall indeed be the Zalimun (disbelievers).

94.author: Itani
94.text: Whoever forges lies about God after that—these are the unjust.

94.author: Maududi
94.text: Those who falsely fix lies upon Allah despite this are the wrong-doers.

94.author: Mubarakpuri
94.text: Then after that, whosoever shall invent a lie against Allah, then it is these that are the wrongdoers.

94.author: Pickthall
94.text: And whoever shall invent a falsehood after that concerning Allah, such will be wrong-doers.

94.author: Qarai
94.text: So whoever fabricates a lie against Allah after that—it is they who are the wrongdoers.

94.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
94.text: Those who after this invent lies about Allah are harmdoers.

94.author: Saheeh International
94.text: And whoever invents about Allah untruth after that - then those are [truly] the wrongdoers.

94.author: Sarwar
94.text: Whoever ascribes falsehood to God despite of all the evidence is unjust.

94.author: Shakir
94.text: Then whoever fabricates a lie against Allah after this, these it is that are the unjust.

94.author: Wahiduddin Khan
94.text: Those who, after this, persist in making up lies and attributing them to God are transgressors."

94.author: Yusuf Ali
94.text: If any, after this, invent a lie and attribute it to Allah, they are indeed unjust wrong-doers.

94.author: Transliteration
94.text: Famani iftara AAala Allahi alkathiba min baAAdi thalika faolaika humu althalimoona

95.author: Ahmed Ali
95.text: Say: "God has veritably spoken the truth. So now follow the way of Abraham the upright, who was not of idolaters."

95.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
95.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Allah is truthful; so follow the religion of Ibrahim, who was free from all falsehood; and he was not of the polytheists.”

95.author: Arberry
95.text: Say: 'God has spoken the truth; therefore follow the creed of Abraham, a man of pure faith and no idolater.'

95.author: Daryabadi
95.text: Say thou: Allah hath spoken the truth; follow therefore the faith of Ibrahim the upright; and he was not of the associators.

95.author: Hilali & Khan
95.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Allah has spoken the truth; follow the religion of Ibrahim (Abraham) Hanifa (Islamic Monotheism, i.e. he used to worship Allah Alone), and he was not of Al-Mushrikun." (See V. 2:105)

95.author: Itani
95.text: Say, “God has spoken the truth, so follow the religion of Abraham the Monotheist; he was not a Pagan.”

95.author: Maududi
95.text: Say: 'Whatever Allah has said is true. Follow, then, the way of Abraham in total devotion to Allah. He was not one of those who associate others with Allah in His divinity.

95.author: Mubarakpuri
95.text: Say:"Allah has spoken the truth; follow the religion of Ibrahim the Hanif (monotheist), and he was not of the Mushrikin (idolators)."

95.author: Pickthall
95.text: Say: Allah speaketh truth. So follow the religion of Abraham, the upright. He was not of the idolaters.

95.author: Qarai
95.text: Say, ‘Allah has spoken the truth; so follow the creed of Abraham, a Hanif, and he was not one of the polytheists.

95.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
95.text: Say: 'Allah has said the truth. Follow the Creed of Abraham, he was of pure faith, and not an idolater'

95.author: Saheeh International
95.text: Say, "Allah has told the truth. So follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth; and he was not of the polytheists."

95.author: Sarwar
95.text: (Muhammad), say, "God has spoken the Truth. Follow the upright tradition of Abraham who was not an idolater."

95.author: Shakir
95.text: Say: Allah has spoken the truth, therefore follow the religion of Ibrahim, the upright one; and he was not one of the polytheists.

95.author: Wahiduddin Khan
95.text: Say, "God speaks the Truth, so follow the faith of Abraham. He was an upright man and he was not one of the polytheists."

95.author: Yusuf Ali
95.text: Say: "Allah speaketh the Truth: follow the religion of Abraham, the sane in faith; he was not of the Pagans."

95.author: Transliteration
95.text: Qul sadaqa Allahu faittabiAAoo millata ibraheema haneefan wama kana mina almushrikeena

96.author: Ahmed Ali
96.text: The first House of God to be set up for men was at Bakkah the blessed, a guidance for the people of the world.

96.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
96.text: Indeed the first house that was appointed as a place of worship for mankind, is the one at Mecca (the Holy Ka’aba), blessed and a guidance to the whole world;

96.author: Arberry
96.text: The first House established for the people was that at Bekka, a place holy, and a guidance to all beings.

96.author: Daryabadi
96.text: Verily the first House set apart unto mankind was that at Bakka, blest, and a guidance unto the worlds.

96.author: Hilali & Khan
96.text: Verily, the first House (of worship) appointed for mankind was that at Bakkah (Makkah), full of blessing, and a guidance for Al-'Alamin (the mankind and jinns).

96.author: Itani
96.text: The first house established for mankind is the one at Bekka; blessed, and guidance for all people.

96.author: Maududi
96.text: Behold, the first House (of Prayer) established for mankind is the one at Bakkah: it is full of blessing and a centre of guidance for the whole world.

96.author: Mubarakpuri
96.text: Verily, the first House (of worship) appointed for mankind was that at Bakkah (Makkah), full of blessing, and a guidance for Al-`Alamin (mankind and Jinn).

96.author: Pickthall
96.text: Lo! the first Sanctuary appointed for mankind was that at Becca, a blessed place, a guidance to the peoples;

96.author: Qarai
96.text: Indeed the first house to be set up for mankind is the one at Bakkah, blessed and a guidance for all nations.

96.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
96.text: The first House ever to be built for people was that at Bakkah (Mecca) blessed and a guidance for the worlds.

96.author: Saheeh International
96.text: Indeed, the first House [of worship] established for mankind was that at Makkah - blessed and a guidance for the worlds.

96.author: Sarwar
96.text: The first house (of worship) that God assigned to men was in Bakka (another name of Mecca). It is a blessed one and a guide for all people.

96.author: Shakir
96.text: Most surely the first house appointed for men is the one at Bekka, blessed and a guidance for the nations.

96.author: Wahiduddin Khan
96.text: The first House to be built for mankind was the one at Bakkah [Makkah]. It is a blessed place; a source of guidance for the whole world.

96.author: Yusuf Ali
96.text: The first House (of worship) appointed for men was that at Bakka: Full of blessing and of guidance for all kinds of beings:

96.author: Transliteration
96.text: Inna awwala baytin wudiAAa lilnnasi lallathee bibakkata mubarakan wahudan lilAAalameena

97.author: Ahmed Ali
97.text: It contains clear signs, and the spot where Abraham had stood. And anyone who enters it will find security. And whosoever can afford should visit the House on a pilgrimage as duty to God. Whosoever denies, should remember that God is independent of the peoples of the world.

97.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
97.text: In it are clear signs – the place where Ibrahim stood (is one of them); and whoever enters it shall be safe; and performing the Hajj (pilgrimage) of this house, for the sake of Allah, is a duty upon mankind, for those who can reach it; and whoever disbelieves – then Allah is Independent (Unwanting) of the entire creation!

97.author: Arberry
97.text: Therein are clear signs -- the station of Abraham, and whosoever enters it is in security. It is the duty of all men towards God to come to the House a pilgrim, if he is able to make his way there. As for the unbeliever, God is All-sufficient nor needs any being.

97.author: Daryabadi
97.text: Therein are signs manifest, the station of Ibrahim. And whosoever entereth it shall be secure. And incumbent on mankind is pilgrimage to the House for that good-will of Allah: on him who is able to find a way thereunto. And whosoever disbelieveth, then verily Allah is Independent of the worlds.

97.author: Hilali & Khan
97.text: In it are manifest signs (for example), the Maqam (place) of Ibrahim (Abraham); whosoever enters it, he attains security. And Hajj (pilgrimage to Makkah) to the House (Ka'bah) is a duty that mankind owes to Allah, those who can afford the expenses (for one's conveyance, provision and residence); and whoever disbelieves [i.e. denies Hajj (pilgrimage to Makkah), then he is a disbeliever of Allah], then Allah stands not in need of any of the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns).

97.author: Itani
97.text: In it are evident signs; the Station of Abraham. Whoever enters it attains security. Pilgrimage to the House is a duty to God for all who can make the journey. But as for those who refuse—God is Independent of the worlds.

97.author: Maududi
97.text: In it there are clear signs and the station of Abraham; whoever enters it becomes secure. Pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by all who can make their way to it. As for those who refuse to follow His command, surely Allah does not stand in need of anything.

97.author: Mubarakpuri
97.text: In it are manifest signs (for example), the Maqam (station) of Ibrahim; whosoever enters it, he attains security. And Hajj to the House is a duty that mankind owes to Allah, for those who are able to undertake the journey; and whoever disbelieves, then Allah stands not in need of any of the `Alamin.

97.author: Pickthall
97.text: Wherein are plain memorials (of Allah's guidance); the place where Abraham stood up to pray; and whosoever entereth it is safe. And pilgrimage to the House is a duty unto Allah for mankind, for him who can find a way thither. As for him who disbelieveth, (let him know that) lo! Allah is Independent of (all) creatures.

97.author: Qarai
97.text: In it are manifest signs [and] Abraham’s Station, and whoever enters it shall be secure. And it is the duty of mankind toward Allah to make pilgrimage to the House—for those who can afford the journey to it—and should anyone renege [on his obligation], Allah is indeed without need of the creatures.

97.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
97.text: In it, there are clear signs; the station where Abraham stood. Whoever enters it let him be safe. Pilgrimage to the House is a duty to Allah for all who can make the journey. And whosoever disbelieves, Allah is Rich, independent of all the worlds.

97.author: Saheeh International
97.text: In it are clear signs [such as] the standing place of Abraham. And whoever enters it shall be safe. And [due] to Allah from the people is a pilgrimage to the House - for whoever is able to find thereto a way. But whoever disbelieves - then indeed, Allah is free from need of the worlds.

97.author: Sarwar
97.text: In (Bakka), there are many clear signs (evidence of the existence of God). Among them is the spot where Abraham stood. Whoever seeks refuge therein will be protected by the laws of amnesty. Those who have the means and ability have a duty to God to visit the House and perform the hajj (pilgrimage) rituals. The unbelievers should know that God is Independent of all creatures.

97.author: Shakir
97.text: In it are clear signs, the standing place of Ibrahim, and whoever enters it shall be secure, and pilgrimage to the House is incumbent upon men for the sake of Allah, (upon) every one who is able to undertake the journey to it; and whoever disbelieves, then surely Allah is Self-sufficient, above any need of the worlds.

97.author: Wahiduddin Khan
97.text: There are clear signs in it; it is the place where Abraham stood. Anyone who enters it will be secure. Pilgrimage to the House is a duty to God for anyone who is able to undertake it. Anyone who disbelieves should remember that God is independent of all creatures.

97.author: Yusuf Ali
97.text: In it are Signs Manifest; (for example), the Station of Abraham; whoever enters it attains security; Pilgrimage thereto is a duty men owe to Allah,- those who can afford the journey; but if any deny faith, Allah stands not in need of any of His creatures.

97.author: Transliteration
97.text: Feehi ayatun bayyinatun maqamu ibraheema waman dakhalahu kana aminan walillahi AAala alnnasi hijju albayti mani istataAAa ilayhi sabeelan waman kafara fainna Allaha ghaniyyun AAani alAAalameena

98.author: Ahmed Ali
98.text: Say: "O people of the Book, why do you reject the word of God when God is a witness to all that you do?"

98.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
98.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “O People given the Book(s)! Why do you not believe in the verses (or signs) of Allah, whereas your deeds are being witnessed by Allah?”

98.author: Arberry
98.text: Say: 'People of the Book, why do you disbelieve in the signs of God? Surely God is witness of the things you do.'

98.author: Daryabadi
98.text: Say thou: O People of the Book! wherefore disbelieve ye in the revelations of Allah, whereas Allah is witness of that which ye work!

98.author: Hilali & Khan
98.text: Say: "O people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians)! Why do you reject the Ayat of Allah (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) while Allah is Witness to what you do?"

98.author: Itani
98.text: Say, “O People of the Scripture, why do you reject the Revelations of God, when God witnesses what you do?”

98.author: Maududi
98.text: Say: 'People of the Book! Why do you reject the signs of Allah when Allah is witness to all that you do?'

98.author: Mubarakpuri
98.text: Say: "O People of the Scripture (Jews and Christians)! Why do you reject the Ayat of Allah, while Allah is Witness to what you do"

98.author: Pickthall
98.text: Say: O People of the Scripture! Why disbelieve ye in the revelations of Allah, when Allah (Himself) is Witness of what ye do?

98.author: Qarai
98.text: Say, ‘O People of the Book! Why do you deny the signs of Allah, while Allah is witness to what you do?’

98.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
98.text: Say: 'People of the Book, why do you disbelieve the verses of Allah? Surely, Allah is witness to all that you do'

98.author: Saheeh International
98.text: Say, "O People of the Scripture, why do you disbelieve in the verses of Allah while Allah is Witness over what you do?"

98.author: Sarwar
98.text: (Muhammad), ask the People of the Book, "Why do you deny the revelations of God when He is Well-Aware of your dealings?".

98.author: Shakir
98.text: Say: O followers of the Book! why do you disbelieve in the communications of Allah? And Allah is a witness of what you do.

98.author: Wahiduddin Khan
98.text: Say, "People of the Book, why do you reject God's revelations when God is witness to all that you do?"

98.author: Yusuf Ali
98.text: Say: "O People of the Book! Why reject ye the Signs of Allah, when Allah is Himself witness to all ye do?"

98.author: Transliteration
98.text: Qul ya ahla alkitabi lima takfuroona biayati Allahi waAllahu shaheedun AAala ma taAAmaloona

99.author: Ahmed Ali
99.text: Then say: "O people of the Book, why do you turn the believers away from the path of God, looking for obliquities in the way when you are witness to it? And God is aware of all that you do."

99.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
99.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “O People given the Book(s)! Why do you prevent those who have accepted faith from the way of Allah, seeking to cause deviation in it, whereas you yourselves are witnesses to it? And Allah is not unaware of your deeds!”

99.author: Arberry
99.text: Say: 'People of the Book, why do you bar from God's way the believer, desiring to make it crooked, yourselves being witnesses? God is not heedless of the things you do.'

99.author: Daryabadi
99.text: Say thou: O people of the Book! wherefore turn ye aside from the way of Allah those who believe, seeking for it crookedness, while ye are witnesses! And Allah is not neglectful of that which ye work.

99.author: Hilali & Khan
99.text: Say: "O people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians)! Why do you stop those who have believed, from the Path of Allah, seeking to make it seem crooked, while you (yourselves) are witnesses [to Muhammad SAW as a Messenger of Allah and Islam (Allah's Religion, i.e. to worship none but Him Alone)]? And Allah is not unaware of what you do."

99.author: Itani
99.text: Say, “O People of the Scripture, why do you hinder from God’s path those who believe, seeking to distort it, even though you are witnesses? God is not unaware of what you do.”

99.author: Maududi
99.text: Say: 'People of the Book! Why do you hinder one who believes from the way of Allah, seeking that he follow a crooked way, even though you yourselves are witness to its being the right way?' Allah is not heedless of what you do.

99.author: Mubarakpuri
99.text: Say: "O People of the Scripture (Jews and Christians)! Why do you stop those who have believed, from the path of Allah, seeking to make it seem crooked, while you (yourselves) are witnesses And Allah is not unaware of what you do."

99.author: Pickthall
99.text: Say: O People of the Scripture! Why drive ye back believers from the way of Allah, seeking to make it crooked, when ye are witnesses (to Allah's guidance)? Allah is not unaware of what ye do.

99.author: Qarai
99.text: Say, ‘O People of the Book! why do you bar the faithful from the way of Allah, seeking to make it crooked, while you are witnesses [to its truthfulness]? And Allah is not oblivious of what you do.’

99.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
99.text: Say: 'People of the Book, why do you bar he who believes from the Path of Allah and seek to make it crooked, when you yourselves are witnesses? Allah is not inattentive of what you do'

99.author: Saheeh International
99.text: Say, "O People of the Scripture, why do you avert from the way of Allah those who believe, seeking to make it [seem] deviant, while you are witnesses [to the truth]? And Allah is not unaware of what you do."

99.author: Sarwar
99.text: Ask them, "Why do you create obstacles in the way of God for those who believe in Him, trying to make His way seem crooked when you know that it is straight? God is not unaware of what you do.

99.author: Shakir
99.text: Say: O followers of the Book! why do you hinder him who believes from the way of Allah? You seek (to make) it crooked, while you are witness, and Allah is not heedless of what you do.

99.author: Wahiduddin Khan
99.text: Say, "People of the Book, why do you turn the believers away from the path of God, seeking to make it crooked, while you are witnesses thereof? God is not unaware of what you do."

99.author: Yusuf Ali
99.text: Say: "O ye People of the Book! Why obstruct ye those who believe, from the path of Allah, Seeking to make it crooked, while ye were yourselves witnesses (to Allah's Covenant)? but Allah is not unmindful of all that ye do."

99.author: Transliteration
99.text: Qul ya ahla alkitabi lima tasuddoona AAan sabeeli Allahi man amana tabghoonaha AAiwajan waantum shuhadao wama Allahu bighafilin AAamma taAAmaloona

100.author: Ahmed Ali
100.text: O believers, if you follow what some of the people of the Book say, it will turn you into unbelievers even after you have come to belief.

100.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
100.text: O People who Believe! If you obey some of People given the Book(s), they will definitely turn you into disbelievers after your having accepted faith.

100.author: Arberry
100.text: O believers, if you obey a sect of those who have been given the Book, they will turn you, after you have believed, into unbelievers.

100.author: Daryabadi
100.text: O Ye who believe! were ye to obey any party amongst those that have been vouchsafed the Book, they would, after your having believed, render you Infidels.

100.author: Hilali & Khan
100.text: O you who believe! If you obey a group of those who were given the Scripture (Jews and Christians), they would (indeed) render you disbelievers after you have believed!

100.author: Itani
100.text: O you who believe! If you obey a party of those who were given the Scripture, they will turn you, after your belief, into disbelievers.

100.author: Maududi
100.text: Believers! Were you to obey a party of those who were given the Book, they might cause you to renounce the Truth after you have attained to faith.

100.author: Mubarakpuri
100.text: O you who believe! If you obey a group of those who were given the Scripture (Jews and Christians), they would (indeed) render you disbelievers after you have believed!

100.author: Pickthall
100.text: O ye who believe! If ye obey a party of those who have received the Scripture they will make you disbelievers after your belief.

100.author: Qarai
100.text: O you who have faith, if you obey a part of those who were given the Book, they will turn you back, after your faith, into faithless ones.

100.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
100.text: Believers, if you obey a sect of those who were given the Book, they will turn you into unbelievers after you have believed.

100.author: Saheeh International
100.text: O you who have believed, if you obey a party of those who were given the Scripture, they would turn you back, after your belief, [to being] unbelievers.

100.author: Sarwar
100.text: Believers, if you obey a certain group among the People of the Book, they will turn you back to disbelief.

100.author: Shakir
100.text: O you who believe! if you obey a party from among those who have been given the Book, they will turn you back as unbelievers after you have believed.

100.author: Wahiduddin Khan
100.text: O believers, if you yield to some of those who were given the Scripture, they will cause you to renounce the truth after you have believed.

100.author: Yusuf Ali
100.text: O ye who believe! If ye listen to a faction among the People of the Book, they would (indeed) render you apostates after ye have believed!

100.author: Transliteration
100.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo in tuteeAAoo fareeqan mina allatheena ootoo alkitaba yaruddookum baAAda eemanikum kafireena

101.author: Ahmed Ali
101.text: And how can you disbelieve? To you are being recited the messages of God, and His prophet is among you. And whosoever holds fast to God shall verily be guided to the path that is straight.

101.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
101.text: And how can you disbelieve, whereas Allah’s verses are recited to you and His Noble Messenger is present amongst you?! And whoever takes the support of Allah is indeed guided to the right path.

101.author: Arberry
101.text: How can you disbelieve, seeing you have God's signs recited to you, and His Messenger among you? Whosoever holds fast to God, he is guided to a straight path.

101.author: Daryabadi
101.text: How can you disbelieve while unto you are recited the revelations of Allah, and in your midst is His apostle! And whosoever holdeth fast to Allah, he is of a surety guided unto a straight path.

101.author: Hilali & Khan
101.text: And how would you disbelieve, while unto you are recited the Verses of Allah, and among you is His Messenger (Muhammad SAW)? And whoever holds firmly to Allah, (i.e. follows Islam Allah's Religion, and obeys all that Allah has ordered, practically), then he is indeed guided to a Right Path.

101.author: Itani
101.text: And how could you disbelieve, when God’s revelations are being recited to you, and among you is His Messenger? Whoever cleaves to God has been guided to a straight path.

101.author: Maududi
101.text: How can you disbelieve when you are the ones to whom the signs of Allah are recited and amidst you is His Messenger? Whoever holds fast to Allah will certainly be guided to the straight way.

101.author: Mubarakpuri
101.text: And how would you disbelieve, while unto you are recited the verses of Allah, and among you is His Messenger And whoever depends upon Allah, then he is indeed guided to the right path.

101.author: Pickthall
101.text: How can ye disbelieve, when it is ye unto whom Allah's revelations are recited, and His messenger is in your midst? He who holdeth fast to Allah, he indeed is guided unto a right path.

101.author: Qarai
101.text: And how would you be faithless while the signs of Allah are recited to you and His Apostle is in your midst? Whoever takes recourse in Allah is certainly guided to a straight path.

101.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
101.text: How can you disbelieve when the verses of Allah are recited to you and His Messenger is among you! He who holds fast to Allah shall be guided to the Straight Path.

101.author: Saheeh International
101.text: And how could you disbelieve while to you are being recited the verses of Allah and among you is His Messenger? And whoever holds firmly to Allah has [indeed] been guided to a straight path.

101.author: Sarwar
101.text: How could you turn back to disbelief when the words of God are recited to you and you have in your midst His Messenger? Those who seek the protection of God will certainly be guided to the right path.

101.author: Shakir
101.text: But how can you disbelieve while it is you to whom the communications of Allah are recited, and among you is His Apostle? And whoever holds fast to Allah, he indeed is guided to the right path.

101.author: Wahiduddin Khan
101.text: But how can you deny the truth when God's revelations are being conveyed to you and His own Messenger is in your midst? He who holds fast to God is indeed guided to the straight path.

101.author: Yusuf Ali
101.text: And how would ye deny Faith while unto you are rehearsed the Signs of Allah, and among you Lives the Messenger? Whoever holds firmly to Allah will be shown a way that is straight.

101.author: Transliteration
101.text: Wakayfa takfuroona waantum tutla AAalaykum ayatu Allahi wafeekum rasooluhu waman yaAAtasim biAllahi faqad hudiya ila siratin mustaqeemin

102.author: Ahmed Ali
102.text: O believers, fear God as He should be feared, and do not die but submitting (to Him).

102.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
102.text: O People who Believe! Fear Allah in the manner He should rightfully be feared, and do not die except as Muslims.

102.author: Arberry
102.text: O believers, fear God as He should be feared, and see you do not die, save in surrender.

102.author: Daryabadi
102.text: O Ye who believe! fear Allah with fear due to Him, and die not except ye be Muslims.

102.author: Hilali & Khan
102.text: O you who believe! Fear Allah (by doing all that He has ordered and by abstaining from all that He has forbidden) as He should be feared. [Obey Him, be thankful to Him, and remember Him always], and die not except in a state of Islam (as Muslims) with complete submission to Allah.

102.author: Itani
102.text: O you who believe! Revere God with due reverence, and do not die except as Muslims.

102.author: Maududi
102.text: Believers! Fear Allah as He should be feared, and see that you do not die save in the state of submission to Allah.

102.author: Mubarakpuri
102.text: O you who believe! Have Taqwa of Allah as is His due, and die not except as (true) Muslims.

102.author: Pickthall
102.text: O ye who believe! Observe your duty to Allah with right observance, and die not save as those who have surrendered (unto Him)

102.author: Qarai
102.text: O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah with the wariness due to Him and do not die except as Muslims.

102.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
102.text: Believers, fear Allah as He should be feared, and do not die except as Muslims.

102.author: Saheeh International
102.text: O you who have believed, fear Allah as He should be feared and do not die except as Muslims [in submission to Him].

102.author: Sarwar
102.text: Believers, have fear of God as you should and die only as Muslims (having submitted to the will of God).

102.author: Shakir
102.text: O you who believe! be careful of (your duty to) Allah with the care which is due to Him, and do not die unless you are Muslims.

102.author: Wahiduddin Khan
102.text: Believers, fear God as is His due, and when death comes, be in a state of complete submission to Him.

102.author: Yusuf Ali
102.text: O ye who believe! Fear Allah as He should be feared, and die not except in a state of Islam.

102.author: Transliteration
102.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo ittaqoo Allaha haqqa tuqatihi wala tamootunna illa waantum muslimoona

103.author: Ahmed Ali
103.text: Hold on firmly together to the rope of God, and be not divided among yourselves, and remember the favours God bestowed on you when you were one another's foe and He reconciled your hearts, and you turned into brethren through His grace. You had stood on the edge of a pit of fire and He saved you from it, thus revealing to you His clear signs that you may find the right way perchance.

103.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
103.text: And hold fast to the rope of Allah, all of you together, and do not be divided; and remember Allah’s favour on you, that when there was enmity between you, He created affection between your hearts, so due to His grace you became like brothers to each other; and you were on the edge of a pit of fire (hell), so He saved you from it; this is how Allah explains His verses to you, so that you may be guided.

103.author: Arberry
103.text: And hold you fast to God's bond, together, and do not scatter; remember God's blessing upon you when you were enemies, and He brought your hearts together, so that by His blessing you became brothers. You were upon the brink of a pit of Fire, and He delivered you from it; even so God makes clear to you His signs; so haply you will be guided.

103.author: Daryabadi
103.text: And hold ye fast, all of you, to the cord of Allah, so and separate not. And remember Allah's favour unto you in that ye were enemies and He joined your hearts together, so ye became by His favour brethren; and ye were on the brink of an abyss of the Fire and He rescued you therefrom. In this wise Allah expoundeth unto you His revelations that haply ye may remain guided.

103.author: Hilali & Khan
103.text: And hold fast, all of you together, to the Rope of Allah (i.e. this Quran), and be not divided among yourselves, and remember Allah's Favour on you, for you were enemies one to another but He joined your hearts together, so that, by His Grace, you became brethren (in Islamic Faith), and you were on the brink of a pit of Fire, and He saved you from it. Thus Allah makes His Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.,) clear to you, that you may be guided.

103.author: Itani
103.text: And hold fast to the rope of God, altogether, and do not become divided. And remember God’s blessings upon you; how you were enemies, and He reconciled your hearts, and by His grace you became brethren. And you were on the brink of a pit of fire, and He saved you from it. God thus clarifies His revelations for you, so that you may be guided.

103.author: Maududi
103.text: Hold fast together to the cable of Allah and be not divided. Remember the blessing that Allah bestowed upon you: you were once enemies then He brought your hearts together, so that through His blessing you became brothers. You stood on the brink of a pit of fire and He delivered you from it. Thus Allah makes His signs clear to you that you may be guided to the right way.

103.author: Mubarakpuri
103.text: And hold fast, all of you together, to the Rope of Allah, and be not divided among yourselves, and remember Allah's favor on you, for you were enemies of one another but He joined your hearts together, so that, by His grace, you became brethren, and you were on the brink of a pit of Fire, and He saved you from it. Thus Allah makes His Ayat clear to you, that you may be guided.

103.author: Pickthall
103.text: And hold fast, all of you together, to the cable of Allah, and do not separate. And remember Allah's favour unto you: How ye were enemies and He made friendship between your hearts so that ye became as brothers by His grace; and (how) ye were upon the brink of an abyss of fire, and He did save you from it. Thus Allah maketh clear His revelations unto you, that haply ye may be guided,

103.author: Qarai
103.text: Hold fast, all together, to Allah’s cord, and do not be divided [into sects]. Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when you were enemies, then He brought your hearts together, so you became brothers with His blessing. And you were on the brink of a pit of Fire, whereat He saved you from it. Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you so that you may be guided.

103.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
103.text: And hold fast to the Bond of Allah, together, and do not scatter. Remember the Favor of Allah bestowed upon you when you were enemies, and how He united your hearts, so that by His Favor you became brothers. And how He saved you from the Pit of Fire when you were on the brink of it. And so Allah makes plain to you His verses, in order that you will be guided.

103.author: Saheeh International
103.text: And hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and do not become divided. And remember the favor of Allah upon you - when you were enemies and He brought your hearts together and you became, by His favor, brothers. And you were on the edge of a pit of the Fire, and He saved you from it. Thus does Allah make clear to you His verses that you may be guided.

103.author: Sarwar
103.text: All of you united hold fast to the rope of God (the Quran and His Messenger), and recall how He favored you when your hostility to each other had torn you apart. He united your hearts in one faith and through His Grace you became brothers. You were on the verge of falling headlong into the abyss of fire, but God saved you. This is how God explains to you His revelations so that you may have the right guidance.

103.author: Shakir
103.text: And hold fast by the covenant of Allah all together and be not disunited, and remember the favor of Allah on you when you were enemies, then He united your hearts so by His favor you became brethren; and you were on the brink of a pit of fire, then He saved you from it, thus does Allah make clear to you His communications that you may follow the right way.

103.author: Wahiduddin Khan
103.text: Hold fast to the cord of God and let nothing divide you. Remember the blessings He has bestowed upon you; you were enemies and then He united your hearts and by His grace you became brothers; you were on the brink of an abyss of Fire and He rescued you from it. Thus God makes His signs clear to you, so that you may find guidance.

103.author: Yusuf Ali
103.text: And hold fast, all together, by the rope which Allah (stretches out for you), and be not divided among yourselves; and remember with gratitude Allah's favour on you; for ye were enemies and He joined your hearts in love, so that by His Grace, ye became brethren; and ye were on the brink of the pit of Fire, and He saved you from it. Thus doth Allah make His Signs clear to you: That ye may be guided.

103.author: Transliteration
103.text: WaiAAtasimoo bihabli Allahi jameeAAan wala tafarraqoo waothkuroo niAAmata Allahi AAalaykum ith kuntum aAAdaan faallafa bayna quloobikum faasbahtum biniAAmatihi ikhwanan wakuntum AAala shafa hufratin mina alnnari faanqathakum minha kathalika yubayyinu Allahu lakum ayatihi laAAallakum tahtadoona

104.author: Ahmed Ali
104.text: So let there be a body among you who may call to the good, enjoin what is esteemed and forbid what is odious. They are those who will be successful.

104.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
104.text: And there should be a group among you that invites to goodness, and enjoins good deeds and forbids immorality; it is they who are the successful.

104.author: Arberry
104.text: Let there be one nation of you, calling to good, and bidding to honour, and forbidding dishonour; those are the prosperers.

104.author: Daryabadi
104.text: And let there be of you a community calling others to good and commanding that which is reputable and prohibiting that which is disreputable. And these they are the blissful.

104.author: Hilali & Khan
104.text: Let there arise out of you a group of people inviting to all that is good (Islam), enjoining Al-Ma'ruf (i.e. Islamic Monotheism and all that Islam orders one to do) and forbidding Al-Munkar (polytheism and disbelief and all that Islam has forbidden). And it is they who are the successful.

104.author: Itani
104.text: And let there be among you a community calling to virtue, and advocating righteousness, and deterring from evil. These are the successful.

104.author: Maududi
104.text: And from among you there must be a party who invite people to all that is good and enjoin the doing of all that is right and forbid the doing of all that is wrong. It is they who will attain true success.

104.author: Mubarakpuri
104.text: Let there arise out of you a group of people inviting to all that is good (Islam), enjoining Al-Ma`ruf (all that Islam orders) and forbidding Al-Munkar (all that Islam has forbidden). And it is they who are the successful.

104.author: Pickthall
104.text: And there may spring from you a nation who invite to goodness, and enjoin right conduct and forbid indecency. Such are they who are successful.

104.author: Qarai
104.text: There has to be a nation among you summoning to the good, bidding what is right, and forbidding what is wrong. It is they who are the felicitous.

104.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
104.text: Let there be one nation of you that shall call to righteousness, ordering honor, and forbidding dishonor. Those are the prosperous.

104.author: Saheeh International
104.text: And let there be [arising] from you a nation inviting to [all that is] good, enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong, and those will be the successful.

104.author: Sarwar
104.text: Let there be a group among you who will invite others to do good deeds, command them to obey the Law, and prohibit them from committing sins. These people will have eternal happiness.

104.author: Shakir
104.text: And from among you there should be a party who invite to good and enjoin what is right and forbid the wrong, and these it is that shall be successful.

104.author: Wahiduddin Khan
104.text: Let there be a group among you who call others to good, and enjoin what is right, and forbid what is wrong: those who do this shall be successful.

104.author: Yusuf Ali
104.text: Let there arise out of you a band of people inviting to all that is good, enjoining what is right, and forbidding what is wrong: They are the ones to attain felicity.

104.author: Transliteration
104.text: Waltakun minkum ommatun yadAAoona ila alkhayri wayamuroona bialmaAAroofi wayanhawna AAani almunkari waolaika humu almuflihoona

105.author: Ahmed Ali
105.text: So be not like those who became disunited and differed among themselves after clear proofs had come to them. For them is great suffering.

105.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
105.text: And do not be like those who became divided and disputed after the clear signs had come to them; and for them is a terrible punishment.

105.author: Arberry
105.text: Be not as those who scattered and fell into variance after the clear signs came to them; those there awaits a mighty chastisement,

105.author: Daryabadi
105.text: And be not like unto those who separated and differed among themselves after there had come unto them evidences. These! for them shall be a torment mighty.

105.author: Hilali & Khan
105.text: And be not as those who divided and differed among themselves after the clear proofs had come to them. It is they for whom there is an awful torment.

105.author: Itani
105.text: And do not be like those who separated and disputed after the clear proofs came to them; for them is a great punishment.

105.author: Maududi
105.text: Do not be like those who fell into factions and differed among themselves after clear signs had come to them. A mighty chastisement awaits them on the Day when some faces will turn bright and ther faces will turn dark. Those whose faces have turned dark will be told: 'Did you fall into unbelief after you had been blessed with belief? Taste, then, chastise- ment for your unbelief.' And those whose faces have turned bright, they will be in the mercy of Allah, and therein they shall abide. These are the mes sages of Allah which We recite to you in truth, and Allah desires no wrong to the people of the world. To Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth, and to Allah are all matters referred for decision.

105.author: Mubarakpuri
105.text: And be not as those who divided and differed among themselves after the clear proofs had come to them. It is they for whom there is an awful torment.

105.author: Pickthall
105.text: And be ye not as those who separated and disputed after the clear proofs had come unto them. For such there is an awful doom,

105.author: Qarai
105.text: Do not be like those who became divided [into sects] and differed after manifest signs had come to them. For such there will be a great punishment

105.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
105.text: Do not follow the example of those who became divided and differed with one another after clear proofs had come to them. For those, there is a great punishment.

105.author: Saheeh International
105.text: And do not be like the ones who became divided and differed after the clear proofs had come to them. And those will have a great punishment.

105.author: Sarwar
105.text: Do not be like those who turned into quarrelling sects after receiving clear authoritative evidence. They will suffer a great torment.

105.author: Shakir
105.text: And be not like those who became divided and disagreed after clear arguments had come to them, and these it is that shall have a grievous chastisement.

105.author: Wahiduddin Khan
105.text: Do not be like those who, after they had been given clear evidence, split into factions and differed among themselves: a terrible punishment awaits such people.

105.author: Yusuf Ali
105.text: Be not like those who are divided amongst themselves and fall into disputations after receiving Clear Signs: For them is a dreadful penalty,-

105.author: Transliteration
105.text: Wala takoonoo kaallatheena tafarraqoo waikhtalafoo min baAAdi ma jaahumu albayyinatu waolaika lahum AAathabun AAatheemun

106.author: Ahmed Ali
106.text: On the Day when some faces would be bright, and some others will be black (with despair), those with black faces (will be told): "Having come to the faith you denied it; now taste therefore the penalty for you disbelieved."

106.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
106.text: On the Day (of Resurrection) when some faces will be shining and some faces black; so, (to) those whose faces are blackened, “What! You disbelieved after you had accepted faith! Therefore now taste the punishment, the result of your disbelief.”

106.author: Arberry
106.text: the day when some faces are blackened, and some faces whitened. As for those whose faces are blackened -- 'Did you disbelieve after you had believed? Then taste the chastisement for that you disbelieved!'

106.author: Daryabadi
106.text: On a day whereon faces become whitened and faces become blackened. Then as for those whose faces shall have become blackened: disbelieved ye after your profession of belief! taste the torment for that ye have been disbelieving.

106.author: Hilali & Khan
106.text: On the Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) when some faces will become white and some faces will become black; as for those whose faces will become black (to them will be said): "Did you reject Faith after accepting it? Then taste the torment (in Hell) for rejecting Faith."

106.author: Itani
106.text: On the Day when some faces will be whitened, and some faces will be blackened. As for those whose faces are blackened: “Did you disbelieve after your belief?” Then taste the punishment for having disbelieved.

106.author: Maududi
106.text: On the Day when some faces will turn bright and ther faces will turn dark. Those whose faces have turned dark will be told: 'Did you fall into unbelief after you had been blessed with belief? Taste, then, chastisement for your unbelief.

106.author: Mubarakpuri
106.text: On the Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) when some faces will become white and some faces will become black; as for those whose faces will become black (to them will be said): "Did you reject faith after accepting it Then taste the torment (in Hell) for rejecting faith."

106.author: Pickthall
106.text: On the Day when (some) faces will be whitened and (some) faces will be blackened; and as for those whose faces have been blackened, it will be said unto them: Disbelieved ye after your (profession of) belief? Then taste the punishment for that ye disbelieved.

106.author: Qarai
106.text: on the day when [some] faces will turn white and [some] faces will turn black. As for those whose faces turn black [it will be said to them], ‘Did you disbelieve after your faith? So taste the punishment because of what you used to disbelieve.’

106.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
106.text: The Day when faces will be whitened and faces blackened. To those whose faces have become blackened it will be said: 'Did you disbelieve after you had believed? Then taste the punishment for that you disbelieved'

106.author: Saheeh International
106.text: On the Day [some] faces will turn white and [some] faces will turn black. As for those whose faces turn black, [to them it will be said], "Did you disbelieve after your belief? Then taste the punishment for what you used to reject."

106.author: Sarwar
106.text: On the day when some faces will become white and others black, God will ask the people with the faces which have become black, "Why did you give up your faith? Now suffer the torment for your disbelief".

106.author: Shakir
106.text: On the day when (some) faces shall turn white and (some) faces shall turn black; then as to those whose faces turn black: Did you disbelieve after your believing? Taste therefore the chastisement because you disbelieved.

106.author: Wahiduddin Khan
106.text: On the Day when some faces are bright and some faces are dark, it will be said to those with darkened faces, "Did you reject faith after accepting it? Taste, then, this punishment for having denied the truth!"

106.author: Yusuf Ali
106.text: On the Day when some faces will be (lit up with) white, and some faces will be (in the gloom of) black: To those whose faces will be black, (will be said): "Did ye reject Faith after accepting it? Taste then the penalty for rejecting Faith."

106.author: Transliteration
106.text: Yawma tabyaddu wujoohun wataswaddu wujoohun faamma allatheena iswaddat wujoohuhum akafartum baAAda eemanikum fathooqoo alAAathaba bima kuntum takfuroona

107.author: Ahmed Ali
107.text: And those with bright faces shall be under God's grace and enjoy it for ever.

107.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
107.text: And those whose faces will be shining, are in the mercy of Allah; they will abide in it forever.

107.author: Arberry
107.text: But as for those whose faces are whitened, they shall be in God's mercy, therein dwelling forever.

107.author: Daryabadi
107.text: And as for those whose faces shall have become whitened-- they shall be in Allah's mercy; therein they shall be abiders.

107.author: Hilali & Khan
107.text: And for those whose faces will become white, they will be in Allah's Mercy (Paradise), therein they shall dwell forever.

107.author: Itani
107.text: But as for those whose faces are whitened: they are in God’s mercy, remaining in it forever.

107.author: Maududi
107.text: And those whose faces have turned bright, they will be in the mercy of Allah, and therein they shall abide.

107.author: Mubarakpuri
107.text: And for those whose faces will become white, they will be in Allah's mercy (Paradise), therein they shall dwell forever.

107.author: Pickthall
107.text: And as for those whose faces have been whitened, in the mercy of Allah they dwell for ever.

107.author: Qarai
107.text: But as for those whose faces become white, they shall dwell in Allah’s mercy, and they will remain in it [forever].

107.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
107.text: As for those whose faces will be whitened, they shall be in the Mercy of Allah for ever.

107.author: Saheeh International
107.text: But as for those whose faces will turn white, [they will be] within the mercy of Allah. They will abide therein eternally.

107.author: Sarwar
107.text: The people whose faces have become white will enjoy the mercy of God with which they will live forever.

107.author: Shakir
107.text: And as to those whose faces turn white, they shall be in Allah's mercy; in it they shall-abide.

107.author: Wahiduddin Khan
107.text: But as for those with shining faces, they shall abide forever in God's grace.

107.author: Yusuf Ali
107.text: But those whose faces will be (lit with) white,- they will be in (the light of) Allah's mercy: therein to dwell (for ever).

107.author: Transliteration
107.text: Waamma allatheena ibyaddat wujoohuhum fafee rahmati Allahi hum feeha khalidoona

108.author: Ahmed Ali
108.text: These are the commandments of God We recite to you verily; God does not wish injustice to the creatures of the world.

108.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
108.text: These are the verses of Allah, which We recite to you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), with truth; and Allah does not wish any injustice to the creation.

108.author: Arberry
108.text: These are the signs of God We recite to thee in truth, and God desires not any injustice to living beings.

108.author: Daryabadi
108.text: These are revelations of Allah; We rehearse them unto thee with truth, and Allah intendeth not wrong unto the worlds.

108.author: Hilali & Khan
108.text: These are the Verses of Allah: We recite them to you (O Muhammad SAW) in truth, and Allah wills no injustice to the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns).

108.author: Itani
108.text: These are the revelations of God. We recite them to you in truth. God desires no injustice for mankind.

108.author: Maududi
108.text: These are the mes sages of Allah which We recite to you in truth, and Allah desires no wrong to the people of the world.

108.author: Mubarakpuri
108.text: These are the Ayat of Allah. We recite them to you in truth, and Allah wills no injustice to the `Alamin.

108.author: Pickthall
108.text: These are revelations of Allah. We recite them unto thee in truth. Allah willeth no injustice to (His) creatures.

108.author: Qarai
108.text: These are the signs of Allah, which We recite to you in truth, and Allah does not desire any wrong for the creatures.

108.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
108.text: Such are the verses of Allah, We recite them to you with truth. Allah does not want injustice for the worlds.

108.author: Saheeh International
108.text: These are the verses of Allah. We recite them to you, [O Muhammad], in truth; and Allah wants no injustice to the worlds.

108.author: Sarwar
108.text: Such are God's revelations which We explain to you, (Muhammad), for a genuine purpose. God does not want injustice for any of His creatures.

108.author: Shakir
108.text: These are the communications of Allah which We recite to you with truth, and Allah does not desire any injustice to the creatures.

108.author: Wahiduddin Khan
108.text: These are God's revelations; We recite them to you in all truth. God desires no injustice to mankind.

108.author: Yusuf Ali
108.text: These are the Signs of Allah: We rehearse them to thee in Truth: And Allah means no injustice to any of His creatures.

108.author: Transliteration
108.text: Tilka ayatu Allahi natlooha AAalayka bialhaqqi wama Allahu yureedu thulman lilAAalameena

109.author: Ahmed Ali
109.text: For to God belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth, and to God do all things return.

109.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
109.text: And to Allah only belongs all whatever is in the heavens and all whatever is in the earth; and towards Allah only is the return of all matters.

109.author: Arberry
109.text: To God belongs all that is in the heavens and in the earth, and unto Him all matters are returned.

109.author: Daryabadi
109.text: Allah's is whatsoever is in the heavens and in the earth; and unto Allah shall be committed all affairs.

109.author: Hilali & Khan
109.text: And to Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth. And all matters go back (for decision) to Allah.

109.author: Itani
109.text: To God belongs everything in the heavens and everything on earth, and to God all events are referred.

109.author: Maududi
109.text: To Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth, and to Allah are all matters referred for decision.

109.author: Mubarakpuri
109.text: And to Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth. And all matters go back (for decision) to Allah.

109.author: Pickthall
109.text: Unto Allah belongeth whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth; and unto Allah all things are returned.

109.author: Qarai
109.text: To Allah belongs whatever there is in the heavens and the earth, and to Allah all matters are returned.

109.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
109.text: To Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth. To Him all matters shall return.

109.author: Saheeh International
109.text: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And to Allah will [all] matters be returned.

109.author: Sarwar
109.text: To God belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth and to Him do all things return.

109.author: Shakir
109.text: And whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth is Allah's; and to Allah all things return

109.author: Wahiduddin Khan
109.text: His is all that the heavens and the earth contain. To God shall all things return.

109.author: Yusuf Ali
109.text: To Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and on earth: To Him do all questions go back (for decision).

109.author: Transliteration
109.text: Walillahi ma fee alssamawati wama fee alardi waila Allahi turjaAAu alomooru

110.author: Ahmed Ali
110.text: Of all the communities raised among men you are the best, enjoining the good, forbidding the wrong, and believing in God. If the people of the Book had come to believe it was best for them; but only some believe, and transgressors are many.

110.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
110.text: You are the best among all the nations that were raised among mankind – you enjoin good deeds and forbid immorality and you believe in Allah; and if the People given the Book(s) believed it would be better for them; some of them are believers (Muslims) and most of them are disbelievers. (The best Ummah is that of Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him.)

110.author: Arberry
110.text: You are the best nation ever brought forth to men, bidding to honour, and forbidding dishonour, and believing in God. Had the People of the Book believed, it were better for them; some of them are believers, but the most of them are ungodly.

110.author: Daryabadi
110.text: O Ye are the best community sent forth unto mankind; ye command that which is reputable and ye prevent that which is disreputable. and ye believe in Allah If the people of the Book believe, surely it were better for them. Of them are believers, and most of them are transgressors.

110.author: Hilali & Khan
110.text: You [true believers in Islamic Monotheism, and real followers of Prophet Muhammad SAW and his Sunnah (legal ways, etc.)] are the best of peoples ever raised up for mankind; you enjoin Al-Ma'ruf (i.e. Islamic Monotheism and all that Islam has ordained) and forbid Al-Munkar (polytheism, disbelief and all that Islam has forbidden), and you believe in Allah. And had the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) believed, it would have been better for them; among them are some who have faith, but most of them are Al-Fasiqun (disobedient to Allah - and rebellious against Allah's Command).

110.author: Itani
110.text: You are the best community that ever emerged for humanity: you advocate what is moral, and forbid what is immoral, and believe in God. Had the People of the Scripture believed, it would have been better for them. Among them are the believers, but most of them are sinners.

110.author: Maududi
110.text: You are now the best people brought forth for (the guidance and reform of) mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and believe in Allah. Had the People of the Book believed it were better for them. Some of them are believers but most of them are transgressors.

110.author: Mubarakpuri
110.text: You are the best of peoples ever raised up for mankind; you enjoin Al-Ma`ruf (all that Islam has ordained) and forbid Al-Munkar (all that Islam has forbidden), and you believe in Allah. And had the People of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) believed, it would have been better for them; among them are some who have faith, but most of them are Fasiqun (rebellious).

110.author: Pickthall
110.text: Ye are the best community that hath been raised up for mankind. Ye enjoin right conduct and forbid indecency; and ye believe in Allah. And if the People of the Scripture had believed it had been better for them. Some of them are believers; but most of them are evil-livers.

110.author: Qarai
110.text: You are the best nation [ever] brought forth for mankind: you bid what is right and forbid what is wrong, and have faith in Allah. And if the People of the Book had believed, it would have been better for them. Among them [some] are faithful, but most of them are transgressors.

110.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
110.text: You are the best nation ever to be brought forth for people. You order honor and forbid dishonor, and you believe in Allah. Had the People of the Book believed, it would have surely been better for them. Some of them are believers, but most of them are evildoers.

110.author: Saheeh International
110.text: You are the best nation produced [as an example] for mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and believe in Allah.
